items.fix: Standardize or normalize numeric columns in a pipeline before fit_predict; choose eps on scaled space.
items.why: eps is a single radius in distance units — a feature in thousands dominates neighbor counts and makes eps meaningless across dimensions.
items.example: Age (0–100) vs income ($0–$500k) without StandardScaler → neighbors match on income magnitude only.

items.mistake: Copying eps from a tutorial without a k-distance plot on your data
items.fix: Compute sorted k-distances (k = min_samples − 1) on scaled training data; pick eps at the elbow and validate with Cluster Evaluation.
items.why: eps is dataset-specific — the same value on different n, d, or scales produces unrelated cluster counts.
items.example: eps=0.5 works on iris after scaling but yields 100% noise on unscaled housing features.

items.mistake: Setting min_samples = 2 on noisy high-dimensional data
items.fix: Start near min_samples ≥ d + 1 or 2·d; increase if too many tiny clusters appear; decrease only with domain justification.
items.why: Very low min_samples makes almost every point core in dense regions, merging distinct groups; in noise, creates spurious chains.
items.example: min_samples=2 on 50-d embeddings → single cluster spanning the entire cloud.

items.mistake: Expecting DBSCAN to find clusters of wildly different densities with one eps
items.fix: Try HDBSCAN, scale features per region, split preprocessing pipelines, or use methods suited to varying density.
items.why: A global eps neighborhood is either too large (merging sparse and dense groups) or too small (dense group becomes noise).
items.example: Tight urban core + sparse suburbs: one eps either merges them or labels suburbs all as noise.

items.mistake: Reporting cluster quality including noise points as a single cluster
items.fix: Exclude label −1 from internal metrics; inspect noise fraction separately; validate with domain maps and stability checks.
items.why: Noise is intentional output, not a failed assignment — mixing it into silhouette or inertia skews evaluation.
items.example: 40% noise labeled −1 but silhouette computed on all rows → misleadingly low cohesion scores.
